summary: Iteration 15 — Backend-only verification of the new POST /api/real-user-traffic/engine-prewarm endpoint at server.py:3676. Added /app/backend/tests/test_iteration15_rut_engine_prewarm.py — 17/17 pytest passed in 2.05s. Iteration_14 regression also re-run: 18/18 passed. Verified: (a) auth gating — no token → 401, bogus JWT → 401; (b) feature-flag gating — fresh user without 'real_user_traffic' → 403; (c) already_ready branch — admin POST currently returns 200 with body {started:false, already_ready:true, status:'ready', expected_revision:'1148', message:...} because chromium-headless-shell rev 1148 is on disk; (d) idempotency — calling prewarm twice in a row keeps started=False, never flips status away from ready; (e) browser_path NOT leaked in response; (f) GET engine-status remains 'ready' after prewarm POST (no regression); (g) missing → installing → ready flow exercised via direct calls to real_user_traffic.get_engine_status() with PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH redirected and _CHROMIUM_INSTALL_IN_PROGRESS toggled — confirms the same branches the endpoint takes (status=='missing' ⇒ background task scheduled & response.started=True; status=='installing' ⇒ already_installing=True); (h) idempotency primitives verified — _CHROMIUM_INSTALL_LOCK is an asyncio.Lock, _CHROMIUM_INSTALL_IN_PROGRESS is a bool, and get_engine_status() reflects flag flips immediately (no caching). Real ~60s chromium reinstall was deliberately NOT executed (would risk concurrent test breakage) — the missing→installing transition is covered by helper-level tests as recommended in the review_request 'other_misc_info' note.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py::rut_engine_prewarm (lines 3676-3717) — clean implementation. Three branches handled correctly: ready → already_ready, installing → already_installing, otherwise → schedules background task and returns started=True. The lazy import of _ensure_chromium_available inside the function (line 3710) intentionally avoids circular import — good. Auth + feature flag gating identical to engine-status. LGTM.

server.py — using FastAPI BackgroundTasks for the install. This means the install runs AFTER the response is returned (correct for UX) but tied to the request lifecycle of THIS worker; if the worker is recycled mid-install the flag would be cleared. Since _ensure_chromium_available's try/finally toggles the flag inside the asyncio.Lock-protected section, a worker restart still self-heals on next request (binary check + flag will be False). Acceptable trade-off — no action required.

server.py — endpoint relies on a TOCTOU read of _rut_get_engine_status() then schedules the install. Two concurrent prewarm POSTs that arrive while status='missing' will both schedule _ensure_chromium_available, but the helper itself uses _CHROMIUM_INSTALL_LOCK + the in-progress flag, so the SECOND scheduled task short-circuits inside the lock. So the response shape is mildly inconsistent (both clients see started=True) but no double install happens. NON-BLOCKING — could optionally check the flag a second time post-lock to return already_installing for the loser, but it's a cosmetic UX nit since the frontend re-polls /engine-status anyway.
real_user_traffic.py::_CHROMIUM_INSTALL_IN_PROGRESS — toggled inside try/finally. Verified via tests that the flag flip is observed by get_engine_status() with no caching. LGTM.

ENHANCEMENT (non-blocking) — Consider adding a soft per-user rate limit on /engine-prewarm (e.g., 1 req/30s) so a stuck client doesn't spam the endpoint while polling. Currently every POST during install just returns already_installing immediately (cheap), but the rate limit would also dampen log noise.
ENHANCEMENT (non-blocking) — Response message 'Prewarm started — refresh status in a moment' could include the expected duration (~60s) so the frontend can render a progress hint. Cosmetic.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration15_rut_engine_prewarm.py (new — 17 tests across 7 classes: auth gating, feature-flag gating, already_ready happy-path + shape + idempotency, engine-status post-prewarm regression, helper-level missing/installing/ready branch verification, idempotency primitives, and iteration_14 regression sweep)
test_credentials: Admin: admin@trackmaster.local / admin123 via POST /api/admin/login (read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: One throwaway regular user (TEST_rut_prewarm_<uuid>@example.com) registered to verify feature-flag gating returns 403. No other state changes; admin POSTs to prewarm only hit the already_ready short-circuit so no chromium reinstall was triggered.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_iteration15_rut_engine_prewarm.py tests/test_iteration14_rut_engine_status.py -v --tb=short --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/iteration15.xml. Requires REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (read from /app/frontend/.env) and PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH defaulting to /pw-browsers. Admin login is /api/admin/login. The missing→installing→ready transition is covered via direct calls to real_user_traffic.get_engine_status() with monkeypatched PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH + _CHROMIUM_INSTALL_IN_PROGRESS — no real chromium reinstall happens, safe to re-run repeatedly. To exercise the REAL install over HTTP (~60s), an operator would need to: (1) move /pw-browsers/chromium_headless_shell-1148 → /tmp/_save, (2) POST /api/real-user-traffic/engine-prewarm and assert started=True/status='installing', (3) POST again immediately and assert already_installing=True, (4) poll GET engine-status until status='ready', (5) restore. Not done here to avoid risk to concurrent jobs.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature-addition iteration. Endpoint behaves exactly as specified by the review_request: 401 unauth, 403 without feature flag, already_ready branch returns started=False/already_ready=True/expected_revision='1148', missing branch (verified via helper) yields the input state for the started=True/status='installing' server response, installing branch returns already_installing=True. All idempotency primitives in place.
